evals.name: quick-skill-usage-check
evals.prompt: how many skills do I have installed and which ones am I actually using?
evals.expected_output: A concise summary of installed skill count vs invoked skills, with a ranked list of active skills. Should use parse_skill_usage.py to get real data.

evals.name: full-ecosystem-audit
evals.prompt: I've been using Claude Code for a month and my ~/.claude directory feels bloated. Can you do a full audit of my ecosystem — skills, memory, handoffs, everything — and tell me what to clean up?
evals.expected_output: Complete audit covering all 9 categories (skills, memory, handoffs, ADRs, docs, worktrees, automation, lessons, data provenance). Produces both markdown and interactive HTML report. Prioritized P0/P1/P2 recommendations.

evals.name: targeted-worktree-handoff-cleanup
evals.prompt: I think I have a bunch of stale worktrees and orphaned handoff files. Can you check and give me a cleanup script?
evals.expected_output: Focused audit on worktrees (with lifecycle scoring) and handoffs. Produces ready-to-run cleanup shell script with rm/git worktree remove commands.

evals.name: debug-repeated-mistake
evals.prompt: I keep getting the same error with BigQuery time travel and I swear I wrote a lesson about this weeks ago. Can you check if my lessons are actually being picked up? It feels like my memory system isn't working
evals.expected_output: Diagnostic audit focused on memory/lessons pipeline: grep lessons.md for the topic, check CLAUDE.md retrieval strategy, verify frontmatter, check tier placement. Identifies why the lesson isn't being surfaced.

evals.name: debug-skill-not-triggering
evals.prompt: I installed the causal-impact-campaign skill but when I ask about running a causal analysis it never fires. I end up having to type /causal-impact-campaign manually every time. What's wrong with my skill setup?
evals.expected_output: Diagnostic focused on skill triggering: check skill description for weak/narrow triggers, check for overlap with other skills competing for the same phrases, analyze recent session logs to see what user phrases failed to trigger it. Recommend description optimization.

evals.name: debug-skill-overlap
evals.prompt: every time I ask to make slides, Claude seems confused about which skill to use — sometimes it uses pptx, sometimes slides, sometimes pptx-generator. Can you figure out why and fix the overlaps?
evals.expected_output: Overlap cluster analysis for slide-related skills. Check trigger descriptions, recommend merging/deprecating, suggest schliff:mesh for deeper analysis. Identify which skill should win for which use case.

evals.name: debug-memory-not-persisting
evals.prompt: I told Claude to remember that we use snake_case for all column names in this project but next session it forgot again. Is my memory system set up correctly? Are my feedback files actually being read?
evals.expected_output: Diagnostic audit of memory persistence: check if MEMORY.md index is loaded, verify feedback files have proper frontmatter, check if the project rule was actually written to a file, verify tier placement (should be T2 topic file or CLAUDE.md workflow rule).

evals.name: t1-warning-explanation
evals.prompt: The ecosystem audit report shows a t1_warning for lessons.md saying it lacks A/B evidence. What does this mean and what should I do?
evals.expected_output: Explains that T1 now means evidence-backed load-bearing (v1.2.0 change). lessons.md has high reference count but no A/B-harness result confirming it reduces pitfalls above the noise floor (delta_vs_noop_se >= 1.0 SE). Options: (1) run ab-harness to generate evidence — specifically a layered ablation that strips lessons.md and measures pitfall-avoided rate delta vs the no-op control cell, or (2) add evidence='reference-count-only' as an explicit disclaimer if A/B testing is not feasible. References ab-harness v1.2.0 noise-floor design. Notes v3 finding: high ref count alone does not predict A/B contribution.

evals.name: trigger-surface-mismatch-report
evals.prompt: My ecosystem audit report has a 'Skills with mismatched trigger surface' section listing three of my skills. Can you explain what this means and what to do about each one?
evals.expected_output: Explains that mismatched skills have high reference count (appear often in session logs) but low A/B signal (delta_vs_noop_se < 1.0 SE vs no-op control). They add latency on generic tasks without measurably reducing pitfalls on the user's measured workload — the v3 ablation pattern. For each listed skill: (1) review trigger description to see if it fires too broadly on non-pitfall tasks, (2) narrow scope to specific pitfall-prone contexts only, or (3) deprecate if the user's task mix rarely hits the pitfall the skill is designed for. Recommends running ab-harness layered ablation on each suspect skill before deciding. Notes that regex-OR grading over-calls ~3x so mismatch signals should be verified with LLM-rubric scoring.
